# Privacy Policy

**Effective:** May 27, 2026
**Applies to:** All plugins published in the Ten30 Studio Claude Code marketplace at `Ten30studio/claude-plugins`, including The Bookkeeper.

---

## The short version

**These plugins store data locally on your machine. Nothing leaves your computer.** Ten30 Studio does not collect, transmit, store, or sell any data generated by your use of these plugins.

---

## What data is stored, and where

When you use a Ten30 Studio Claude Code plugin, certain data may be written to your local filesystem under directories on your own machine. For example:

- **The Bookkeeper** stores engagement logs, statements, and client metadata under `~/.bookkeeper/` on your local filesystem. This includes service descriptions, dollar values, courtesy amounts, source citations, and any notes you provide.

This data exists on **your computer only**. It is not transmitted to any Ten30 Studio server, third-party server, or any other destination.

## What data Ten30 Studio collects

**None, directly.** Ten30 Studio operates no telemetry, analytics, tracking, or data-collection mechanism within these plugins. We do not know how many people install, use, or uninstall our plugins beyond what GitHub's standard repository statistics show publicly.

## Third-party considerations

These plugins run within **Claude Code**, which is a product of Anthropic. Anthropic's data handling, telemetry, and privacy practices apply to your use of Claude Code itself. Refer to [Anthropic's privacy policy](https://www.anthropic.com/legal/privacy) for those details.

When Claude (via Claude Code) generates output using these plugins, your prompts and Claude's responses pass through Anthropic's infrastructure in the normal course of Claude operation. Ten30 Studio has no special access to or visibility into these interactions.

## Your responsibility for data security

Because all plugin-generated data is stored locally on your machine:

- **Backups are your responsibility.** Ten30 Studio cannot recover data lost due to disk failure, accidental deletion, or other local issues.
- **Access control is your responsibility.** Anyone with access to your user account on your machine can read the data in `~/.bookkeeper/` or any other plugin-generated directory.
- **Compliance is your responsibility.** If you process client data (e.g., business names, financials, contact info) through these plugins, you remain responsible for whatever data protection obligations apply to your use (GDPR, CCPA, industry-specific regulations, etc.).

## Updates to plugins

When you install a Ten30 Studio plugin from this marketplace, you accept that the plugin code may be updated by Ten30 Studio at any time. Updates are pushed to the public GitHub repository at `github.com/Ten30studio/claude-plugins`. The full source code is open and inspectable.

By design, we ship updates publicly so any user can review changes before adopting them. Auto-updates in Claude Code are user-controlled.

## Open source

All Ten30 Studio plugins are MIT-licensed and the source is publicly available at:

**https://github.com/Ten30studio/claude-plugins**

You may inspect the code, audit data flows, or fork the repository at any time.

## Contact

Questions about privacy, data handling, or any other concern related to these plugins:

**Ten30 Studio**
admin@ten30studio.com
https://ten30studio.com

## Changes to this policy

Ten30 Studio may update this privacy policy as our plugin portfolio expands. The current, canonical version is always hosted at:

**https://www.ten30studio.com/privacy**

Material changes will be noted in the repository commit history.
